learning.learning_goal: Understand that 'how many more' asks for the gap between two amounts.
learning.big_idea: Comparison means finding the gap between two amounts.

screens.label: Lesson 001
screens.title: How many more?
screens.body: One idea: comparison means finding the gap between two amounts.

visual.message: Both rows start in the same place. Which row goes farther?

screens.label: Discover
screens.title: What do the glowing blocks show?

visual.message: The extra blocks show how much farther the top row goes.

choices.feedback: The glowing blocks show the gap.

hints: Look at the two rows side by side.
Look for the blocks that do not have a match.
Only count the blocks that do not have a match.

screens.label: Language
screens.title: Different words. Same gap.

visual.message: These phrases all point to the same relationship.

screens.guidance: Some words can be tricky. First ask: are we putting groups together, or comparing them?

screens.label: Symbol
screens.title: What equation measures the gap?

visual.message: The top row goes farther. Which equation measures that gap?

choices.feedback: Start with the larger amount and subtract the smaller amount.

hints: The equation should measure what is extra.
Start with 15 because it is the larger group.

visual.message: The equation confirms what the visual showed.

screens.label: Reflect
screens.title: What did you discover?
screens.prompt: Choose the statement that explains the idea.

choices.text: Subtraction can measure the gap between two amounts.
The word more always means add.

hints: Think about what the blocks showed.

screens.title: You measured the gap.
screens.body: You connected the visual, the words, and the equation.
